results.title: About desktop pets & virtual companions - The Candybox Blog
results.content:
All these more recent examples are not exactly “desktop pets proper” in terms of freely roaming your own desktop. All of these exist in their own separate window, independent of the desktop.  
 I tried really hard to find recent examples, and (other than my own work) I only really found three (Desktop Goose, Desktop Awoo, and Love Squid).  
 Yes. I checked outside of itch too.  
 I don’t know why devs haven’t really been making screen roamers as much as tamagotchi type pets. It might be a point of reference (you make a pet based on what you know, and if you are mostly familiar with tamagotchi, why do roamers?)…  
 I think it also might be the use of technology. Unity doesn’t necessarily yield itself to this as well as other options. You can argue that, but show me examples then. [...] Desktop Goose

Aside from my own work, the most known current example of a desktop pet that properly fits in the traditional category of a DESKTOP pet is Desktop Goose.

Here’s a small story, and this has a happy end, so bear with me… [...] My recommendations are Electric Love Potato 2.0 (A potato companion for your desktop with lots of features. It will make fan art of you), Runonce (A virtual companion that you can only run once and then never again. It’s an existential exercise in loss and love), Frog Pets (Truly weird and Seaman often comes up as a comparison), and Cyberpet Graveyard (A collection of unlovable, and unwanted desktop pets for which something went truly wrong)…

  
 Image from Wikipedia’s Tamagotchi page.

Imagine my delight when I started seeing virtual pets pop up among other indie developers!  
 There are more and more appearing on itch.

results.title: Desktop Pets Tier List 2
results.content: Ball in the C tier. The next desktop pet that I want to open up for you guys, and I'm sure you've heard of it, but it's Clippy. Clippy, officially known as Clip It, is a virtual assistant character introduced by Microsoft in 1996. It's basically a bouncing paperclip designed to assist users with word processing tasks in Microsoft Office. If you had Microsoft Office, like Microsoft Word for example, and you were doing something on Microsoft Word, like making up an essay, you would use Clippy to assist you with these documents. Since Clippy has been officially discontinued, I have a different version that's actually powered by ChatGpt, which is really cool. But you have to give it an API key from chat GPT for it to have artificial intelligence capabilities. Like, I paid for API credits. I [...] actually looks kind of nice. I wish you could customize. If you could customize them, I would have given this an A tier, but since you can't customize them, I'm going to leave it on the B tier next to Blob Buddy. I think they're quite useful. The next desktop pet that we're going to open is called virtual desktop pet. I'm pretty sure it's just a cat. Is it better than Nico? Let's find out. It's a cat. It's It is a cat. So, he's Okay, you click him and you give him some love. He's still Oh, he's happy. There you go. Okay. So, all you can do is just click on the cat. It roams around on your desktop. Now, I'm going to be very fair because I like desktop pets that don't have backgrounds. Like when I opened up my pet ghost or virtual pet rock, those ones were actual windows that were open. But [...] In today's video, I am going to be ranking desktop pets. I'm sure you already know what it is, but I'm going to quickly explain it for you guys. A desktop pet is literally a virtual pet that you could have on your computer. It is technically like your desktop virtual companion. I'm going to be ranking them with a typical tiering system. All right, the first virtual pet that I'm going to be opening up is called Blob Buddy. All I know is that it's kind of a Bonsy Buddy copycat except that it's kind of mean. This is what the website looks like. That's all I got from the website. Let's just go ahead and right click, run as administrator. Yo, it's me, the blob channel. Before we get started, I want to know your name. Also, why are you up so late? Go to bed. No life. You see what I mean about

good starting point for what's to come there's going to be something that's better than bonsy buddy and there's going to be something that's significantly worse than bonsy buddy next up is desktop Goose so desktop Goose plain and simple it's just a goose that that roams around on your desktop there he is we have the desktop Goose now it's a I mean it just walks around Waddles around on your computer it just chills it's pretty cute it's pretty cool what's he going to do if you hold down the escape button you can actually evict the goose from your screen but I'm not going to do that so he should come back where did he go oh he's what is this what are you doing good work thank you Goose what are you doing I closed the notepad and he started going berserk so we have the desktop Goose um [...] oh it pooped oh my God it pooped you tap it by collecting its poop add some food and a ball play with the ball oh that's so hype I added that so you guys can see what I'm doing cuz there's no actual way to record my my touch bar and I have my phone over here to show you guys my actual touch bar in case anything goes wrong but this this is on MacBooks and if you have a MacBook with a touch bar hit the ball oh my God it's pretty hype it's pretty hype I really really really like this one he fell asleep bro just fell asleep and finally Kito pet now Cano pet is not necessarily a desktop companion it's a it's a game on Steam that replicates your desktop and it actually uses a lot of cool ways to make it seem like it's an actual desktop but if you want to see Cano pet in action you can watch my [...] # Desktop Pets Tier List
